ucs.title: [LLM] Bluenoroff SnatchCrypto: ISO/IMG container -> LNK/HTA -> mshta/PowerShell beaconing to Dropbox/OneDrive/Google Drive C2
ucs.description: Detects the canonical SnatchCrypto / TA444 delivery chain: an ISO, IMG, VHD or RAR container is dropped (often via spear-phishing posing as a venture-capital intro or job offer to a crypto/Web3 firm), the user clicks an embedded LNK that launches mshta or PowerShell from the mounted volume, and the loader fetches the next stage from a legitimate cloud-storage provider (Dropbox API, OneDrive, Google Drive, Mega) that Bluenoroff has used as primary staging since 2021. Tying the container-drop, the LOLBin spawn, and the cloud-storage egress into one chain is what makes this Bluenoroff-shaped rath
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_name) as container_name values(Filesystem.file_path) as container_path values(Filesystem.user) as drop_user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_name="*.iso" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.img" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhd" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.vhdx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.rar" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.zip") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Outlook\\*") by host Filesystem.user | rename Filesystem.user as user | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as procTime values(Processes.process_name) as lolbin values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="explorer.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") (Processes.process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process MATCH "(?i)[D-Z]:\\\\.+\\.(hta|ps1|bat|vbs|js|cmd|lnk)") by host Processes.user Processes.process_id] | where procTime>=firstTime AND procTime<=firstTime+3600 | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as netTime values(Web.url) as cloud_url values(Web.dest) as cloud_dest values(Web.dest_port) as cloud_port values(Web.app) as net_proc from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.app IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe") (Web.url="*dropboxusercontent.com*" OR Web.url="*api.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*content.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*googleusercontent.com*" OR Web.url="*drive.google.com*" OR Web.url="*docs.google.com*" OR Web.url="*onedrive.live.com*" OR Web.url="*1drv.ms*" OR Web.url="*sharepoint.com*" OR Web.url="*mega.nz*" OR Web.url="*mediafire.com*" OR Web.url="*pcloud.com*") by host] | where netTime>=procTime AND netTime<=procTime+1800 | table firstTime procTime netTime host user container_name container_path lolbin parent cmdline net_proc cloud_url cloud_dest cloud_port
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let CloudStagingDomains = dynamic(["dropboxusercontent.com","api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com","docs.google.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","mediafire.com","mega.nz","pcloud.com","yandex.com"]);
let CloudStagingProcs = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
// 1. Bluenoroff favours ISO/IMG/RAR mail droppers to bypass MOTW
let ContainerDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".iso" or FileName endswith ".img"
        or FileName endswith ".vhd" or FileName endswith ".vhdx"
        or FileName endswith ".rar" or FileName endswith ".zip"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Outlook\")
    | project ContainerTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ContainerName = FileName, ContainerPath = FolderPath;
// 2. LOLBin spawned by explorer with a LNK / non-C-drive script argument (mounted ISO)
let LolBinSpawns = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (CloudStagingProcs)
    | where (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "explorer.exe"
             and (ProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
                  or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\"))
         or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\[^\\]+\.(hta|ps1|bat|vbs|js|cmd|lnk)\b"
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LolBin = FileName, LolBinCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, LolBinPid = ProcessId;
// 3. Cloud-storage egress from the same host within 30 min
let CloudEgress = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (CloudStagingProcs)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort;
ContainerDrops
| join kind=inner LolBinSpawns on DeviceId
| where ProcTime between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + 1h)
| join kind=inner CloudEgress on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| project ContainerTime, ProcTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          ContainerName, ContainerPath,
          LolBin, LolBinCmd, ParentImage,
          NetProc, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let CloudStagingDomains = dynamic(["dropboxusercontent.com","api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com","docs.google.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","mediafire.com","mega.nz","pcloud.com"]);
let CloudStagingProcs = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let ContainerDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".iso" or TargetFileName endswith ".img"
        or TargetFileName endswith ".vhd" or TargetFileName endswith ".vhdx"
        or TargetFileName endswith ".rar" or TargetFileName endswith ".zip"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Outlook\")
    | project ContainerTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              ContainerName = TargetFileName, ContainerPath = TargetFilePath;
let LolBinSpawns = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
    | where ChildName in (CloudStagingProcs)
    | where (ParentProcessName endswith @"\explorer.exe"
             and (CommandLine has ".lnk"
                  or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\"))
         or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\b[D-Z]:\\[^\\]+\.(hta|ps1|bat|vbs|js|cmd|lnk)\b"
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              LolBin = ChildName, LolBinCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let CloudEgress = CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RequestURL has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
         or DestinationHostName has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, src_host = SourceHostName,
              src_ip = SourceIP, RequestURL, DestinationHostName,
              DestinationIP, DestinationPort;
ContainerDrops
| join kind=inner LolBinSpawns on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where ProcTime between (ContainerTime .. ContainerTime + 1h)
| join kind=inner CloudEgress on $left.Computer == $right.src_host
| where NetTime between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 30m)
| project ContainerTime, ProcTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, ProcTime),
          Computer, Account,
          ContainerName, ContainerPath,
          LolBin, LolBinCmd, ParentProcessName,
          RequestURL, DestinationHostName, DestinationIP, DestinationPort
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Three-event chain (container drop -> mounted-volume LOLBin via explorer LNK -> cloud-storage C2 within 30 min) is uniquely Bluenoroff/SnatchCrypto. Generic LNK or generic ISO drop alone is too noisy; the combination matches Kaspersky/Securelist's documented SnatchCrypto playbook and the 2022-2024 TA444 spear-phishing campaigns where Dropbox/OneDrive were the staging tier. The non-C-drive regex cat

ucs.title: [LLM] Bluenoroff signed-app DLL side-loading from user-writable path with cloud-storage callback (RustBucket / fake-job lure pattern)
ucs.description: Detects Bluenoroff's signature DLL search-order hijack where a legitimately signed third-party binary (SumatraPDF, AnyDesk, Citrix wfica32, PuTTY, OBS, Postman, OneNote) is dropped into a user-writable directory (Downloads/Temp/ProgramData/Public) alongside a malicious sibling DLL that hijacks an import. Bluenoroff has used this exact pattern in the AppleJeus / fake-trader-app and 2023-2024 fake-recruiter campaigns to bypass application allow-listing, then beacons to Dropbox / Google Drive / OneDrive / Telegram for second-stage retrieval.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as loader_path values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("sumatrapdf.exe","anydesk.exe","anydeskmsi.exe","wfica32.exe","javaw.exe","wmplayer.exe","vmnat.exe","onenote.exe","putty.exe","pageant.exe","postman.exe","obs64.exe","oneclickapp.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.user | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as netTime values(Web.url) as cloud_url values(Web.dest) as cloud_dest values(Web.dest_port) as cloud_port values(Web.app) as net_proc values(Web.http_user_agent) as ua from datamodel=Web.Web where (Web.url="*dropboxusercontent*" OR Web.url="*api.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*content.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*googleusercontent*" OR Web.url="*drive.google.com*" OR Web.url="*docs.google.com*" OR Web.url="*onedrive.live.com*" OR Web.url="*1drv.ms*" OR Web.url="*sharepoint.com*" OR Web.url="*api.telegram.org*" OR Web.url="*mega.nz*") by host] | where netTime>=firstTime AND netTime<=firstTime+1800 | table firstTime netTime host user process_name loader_path parent cmdline net_proc cloud_url cloud_dest cloud_port ua
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Public\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Packages\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
// Signed third-party binaries Bluenoroff has been observed weaponising via DLL search order
let SignedHostBinaries = dynamic(["sumatrapdf.exe","anydeskmsi.exe","anydesk.exe","wfica32.exe","javaw.exe","wmplayer.exe","vmnat.exe","onenote.exe","putty.exe","pageant.exe","postman.exe","obs64.exe","oneclickapp.exe"]);
let CloudStagingDomains = dynamic(["dropboxusercontent.com","api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com","docs.google.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","api.telegram.org","mega.nz"]);
// 1. Adjacent unsigned DLL loaded by a signed loader sitting in a user-writable path
let Sideloads = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SignedHostBinaries)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (UserWritablePaths)
    | where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath           // sibling DLL
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
    | project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              SignedHost = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              HostFolder = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              SideloadedDll = FileName, DllPath = FolderPath,
              DllSHA256 = SHA256, LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              LoaderPid = InitiatingProcessId;
// 2. Same loader process speaks to legitimate cloud staging
let CloudCallback = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SignedHostBinaries)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              NetProc = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              NetProcPid = InitiatingProcessId,
              RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort;
Sideloads
| join kind=inner CloudCallback on DeviceId, $left.LoaderPid == $right.NetProcPid
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 30m)
| project LoadTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, LoadTime),
          DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          SignedHost, HostFolder, LoaderSHA256,
          SideloadedDll, DllPath, DllSHA256,
          NetProc, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by LoadTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let UserWritablePaths = dynamic([@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Public\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\"]);
let SignedHostBinaries = dynamic(["sumatrapdf.exe","anydesk.exe","anydeskmsi.exe","wfica32.exe","javaw.exe","wmplayer.exe","vmnat.exe","onenote.exe","putty.exe","pageant.exe","postman.exe","obs64.exe"]);
let CloudStagingDomains = dynamic(["dropboxusercontent.com","api.dropboxapi.com","content.dropboxapi.com","googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com","docs.google.com","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","sharepoint.com","api.telegram.org","mega.nz"]);
let Sideloads = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7    // ImageLoad
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             User = tostring(EventData.User),
             LoaderPid = tolong(EventData.ProcessId),
             ImageHash = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
    | extend ImageName = tolower(tostring(split(Image, @"\")[-1])),
             ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(Image, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\"),
             LoadedDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLoaded, @"\"), 0, -2), @"\")
    | where ImageName in (SignedHostBinaries)
    | where ImageDir has_any (UserWritablePaths)
    | where ImageLoaded endswith ".dll"
    | where LoadedDir =~ ImageDir
    | where not(LoadedDir startswith @"C:\Windows\")
    | project LoadTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, User,
              SignedHost = ImageName, HostFolder = ImageDir,
              SideloadedDll = ImageLoaded, LoaderPid;
let CloudCallback = CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RequestURL has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
         or DestinationHostName has_any (CloudStagingDomains)
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, src_host = SourceHostName,
              RequestURL, DestinationHostName,
              DestinationIP, DestinationPort, RequestClientApplication;
Sideloads
| join kind=inner CloudCallback on $left.Computer == $right.src_host
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 30m)
| project LoadTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, LoadTime),
          Computer, User, SignedHost, HostFolder, SideloadedDll,
          RequestURL, DestinationHostName, DestinationIP, DestinationPort, RequestClientApplication
| order by LoadTime desc
ucs.rationale: DLL side-loading from a signed third-party binary planted in a user-writable folder is the structural fingerprint of Bluenoroff's intrusions documented by Kaspersky (SnatchCrypto), Mandiant (APT38 / TA444), and Jamf (RustBucket on macOS variants). The actor specifically chooses widely-allowlisted apps - PuTTY, AnyDesk, SumatraPDF, OBS - because EDRs and AV trust the parent. Coupling the side-load
